question_text: How does friction affect the forces on a bicycle moving at constant speed?

options.label: A
options.text: Friction is the only force acting on the bicycle

options.label: B
options.text: Friction must be balanced by the driving force to maintain constant speed

options.label: D
options.text: Friction has no effect on the forces

correct_answer: Friction must be balanced by the driving force to maintain constant speed
explanation: When a bicycle moves at constant speed, it is not accelerating. This means the forces acting on it must be balanced. The driving force from pedalling must equal the friction forces (air resistance and rolling resistance) opposing the motion. Balanced forces result in no acceleration, allowing constant velocity motion.
